content.content: {"body":"* **Suggested term label:** tyramine transport * **Definition (free text)** The directed movement of tyramine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Tyramine is a monoamine neurotransmitter occurring predominantly in invertebrates neuronal as well as in non-neuronal tissues. * **Reference, in format PMID:#######** PMID:9870940 (C.elegans) PMID:15849736 (Dmel) PMID:32305263 (review, Dmel) * **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term** Vmat (Dmel) cat-1 (C.elegans) * **Parent term(s)** GO:0015844 monoamine transport * **Children terms (if applicable)** Should any existing terms that should be moved underneath this new proposed term? The existing term GO:0061545 tyramine secretion will be a child of the new term --- * **Suggested term label:** octopamine transport * **Definition (free text)** The directed movement of octopamine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Octopamine is a monoamine neurotransmitter occurring predominantly in invertebrates neuronal as well as in non-neuronal tissues. * **Reference, in format PMID:#######** PMID:9870940 (C.elegans) PMID:15849736 (Dmel) PMID:32305263 (review, Dmel) * **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term** Vmat (Dmel) cat-1 (C.elegans) * **Parent term(s)** GO:0015844 monoamine transport * **Children terms (if applicable)** Should any existing terms that should be moved underneath this new proposed term? The existing term GO:0061539 octopamine secretion will be a child of the new term","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7WCrtm","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please create two new terms: tyramine transport and octopamine transport.","createdAt":"2025-11-29T06:01:09Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3591027558","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7WCsKs","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I've successfully created the two requested transport terms: **GO:7770032 tyramine transport** - Definition: The directed movement of tyramine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Tyramine is a monoamine neurotransmitter occurring predominantly in invertebrates. - Parent: GO:0015844 (monoamine transport) - References: PMID:9870940, PMID:15849736, PMID:32305263 **GO:7770033 octopamine transport** - Definition: The directed movement of octopamine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Octopamine is a monoamine neurotransmitter occurring predominantly in invertebrates. - Parent: GO:0015844 (monoamine transport) - References: PMID:9870940, PMID:15849736, PMID:32305263 Both terms include logical definitions following the standard transport pattern with appropriate ChEBI terms and will serve as parent terms for the existing secretion terms (GO:0061545 tyramine secretion and GO:0061539 octopamine secretion). I've created PR #31144 for review. Please let me know if any adjustments are needed. @dragon-ai-agent","createdAt":"2025-11-29T06:04:04Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3591029420","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7WCtSp","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please revise the pull request, remove is_a GO:0015844 monoamine transport relationships from the new terms, and add GO:0061545 tyramine secretion is_a GO:0015844 monoamine transport and GO:0061539 octopamine secretion is_a octopamine transport.","createdAt":"2025-11-29T06:11:26Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3591034025","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7WCubF","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I've successfully revised PR #31144 according to your request. **Summary of changes:** 1. **Removed** `is_a GO:0015844 (monoamine transport)` from: - GO:7770032 (tyramine transport) - GO:7770033 (octopamine transport) 2. **Added** new parent relationships: - GO:0061545 (tyramine secretion) `is_a` GO:0015844 (monoamine transport) - GO:0061539 (octopamine secretion) `is_a` GO:7770033 (octopamine transport) The new transport terms now rely on their logical definitions (`intersection_of` axioms) to infer the parent relationship to monoamine transport through reasoning. The secretion terms are now properly linked to their corresponding transport processes. All changes have been validated and pushed to the PR branch. @dragon-ai-agent","createdAt":"2025-11-29T06:20:15Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3591038661","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7WGD-X","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"[Term] id: GO:7770032 name: tyramine transport namespace: biological_process def: "The directed movement of tyramine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Tyramine is a monoamine neurotransmitter occurring predominantly in invertebrates." [PMID:9870940, PMID:15849736, PMID:32305263] intersection_of: GO:0006810 ! transport intersection_of: has_primary_input CHEBI:327995 ! tyraminium property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143" xsd:anyURI created_by: dragon-ai-agent [Term] id: GO:7770033 name: octopamine transport namespace: biological_process def: "The directed movement of octopamine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Octopamine is a monoamine neurotransmitter occurring predominantly in invertebrates." [PMID:9870940, PMID:15849736, PMID:32305263] intersection_of: GO:0006810 ! transport intersection_of: has_primary_input CHEBI:58025 ! octopaminium property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143" xsd:anyURI created_by: dragon-ai-agent","createdAt":"2025-11-29T20:16:05Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3591913367","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7WRvGI","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Hi @rozaru @raymond91125 We have been trying to create/keep terms that mention whether the transport is transmembrane (or something else). Is this TM ? (otherwise these terms would be 'do not annotate'). We need to clarify guidelines on transport. Thanks, Pascale ","createdAt":"2025-12-01T07:20:20Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3594973576","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7WS9zP","author":{"login":"rozaru"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Both Vmat and cat-1 are transmembrane transporter that are used to import tyramine and octopamine neurotransmitters into synaptic vesicles. So in that case I would use the new created terms as having transmembrane transporter activity. The other similar terms in this branch don’t mention transmembrane probably because one of the children “neurotransmitter" secretion often doesn’t involve the direct action of a transporter. ``` |__monoamine transport |__catecholamine transport | |__catecholamine secretion | | |__catecholamine secretion, neurotransmission(+) | | |__dopamine secretion(+) | | |__epinephrine secretion(+) | | |__norepinephrine secretion(+) | | |__regulation of catecholamine secretion(+) | |__catecholamine uptake | | |__catecholamine uptake involved in synaptic transmission(+) | | |__dopamine uptake(+) | | |__norepinephrine uptake(+) | |__dopamine transport 4 rec. | | |__dopamine secretion(+) | | |__dopamine uptake(+) | |__epinephrine transport | | |__epinephrine secretion(+) | |__norepinephrine transport | |__norepinephrine secretion(+) | |__norepinephrine uptake(+) |__serotonin transport | |__serotonin secretion | | |__regulation of serotonin secretion(+) | | |__serotonin secretion involved in inflammatory response(+) | | |__serotonin secretion, neurotransmission | |__serotonin uptake | |__regulation of serotonin uptake(+) |__tyramine secretion |__tyramine secretion, neurotransmission ```","createdAt":"2025-12-01T08:42:43Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3595295951","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7Wp2Iq","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@thomaspd and I looked at the neurotransmission part of the ontology, and we are wondering whether you can annotate the TM transporter MF, and use BP '[neurotransmitter loading into synaptic vesicle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098700)' ? Or a child of that ? This would be more clearly describing the actual biology. (Obviously that entire branch needs to be reviewed)","createdAt":"2025-12-02T10:21:02Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3601293866","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7WrNwA","author":{"login":"rozaru"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Thanks for the suggestion, it's more informative. The current annotation for Vmat already includes them but has also all the xx transport BP terms. This is why I asked for the new ones. --- Is it still OK to annotate to the BP transport term? Looking at the tree the 2 types of BP terms are not really linked: ","createdAt":"2025-12-02T11:52:06Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3601652736","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7W1Jt0","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"If we create a leaf term like: 'dopamine loading into synaptic vesicle' with the logical definition "transport and ('has target start location' some cytosol) and ('has target end location' some 'synaptic vesicle lumen') and ('has primary input' some 'dopaminium(1+)') and ('has primary input' some 'substance with neurotransmitter role')" It will have inferred parents 'dopamine transport' and 'neurotransmitter loading into synaptic vesicle'. We can create 'tyramine loading into synaptic vesicle' and 'octopamine loading into synaptic vesicle'. We keep GO:7770032 tyramine transport and GO:7770033 octopamine transport but need not annotate with them directly.","createdAt":"2025-12-02T22:44:32Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3604257652","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7W1ppe","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"+[Term] +id: GO:0160310 +name: dopamine loading into synaptic vesicle +namespace: biological_process +def: "The active transport of dopamine neurotransmitters into a synaptic vesicle." [PMID:15849736, PMID:9870940] +intersection_of: GO:0006810 ! transport +intersection_of: has_primary_input CHEBI:327995 ! tyraminium +intersection_of: has_primary_input GOCHE:25512 ! substance with neurotransmitter role +intersection_of: has_target_end_location GO:0034592 ! synaptic vesicle lumen +intersection_of: has_target_start_location GO:0005829 ! cytosol +property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143" xsd:anyURI +created_by: rynl +creation_date: 2025-12-02T23:16:14Z + +[Term] +id: GO:0160312 +name: octopamine loading into synaptic vesicle +namespace: biological_process +def: "The active transport of octopamine neurotransmitters into a synaptic vesicle." [PMID:15849736, PMID:9870940] +intersection_of: GO:0006810 ! transport +intersection_of: has_primary_input CHEBI:58025 ! octopaminium +intersection_of: has_primary_input GOCHE:25512 ! substance with neurotransmitter role +intersection_of: has_target_end_location GO:0034592 ! synaptic vesicle lumen +intersection_of: has_target_start_location GO:0005829 ! cytosol +property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143" xsd:anyURI +created_by: rynl +creation_date: 2025-12-02T23:21:33Z ","createdAt":"2025-12-02T23:32:28Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3604388446","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7W2YUL","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Please let me know if this is a preferred solution. Thanks.","createdAt":"2025-12-03T01:02:09Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3604579595","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7XCqJj","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I like the idea - but then 1. this should somehow be a transmembrane transport - probably [neurotransmitter loading into synaptic vesicle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0098700)' should have the transmembrane transporter parent (or can we go higher up?) 2. we should look at all neurotransmitters transport and see if the same solution can be applied (at least in @rozaru 's list [here](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3595295951) 3. then we would not need the x transport terms (obsolete?) Does that work ? Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2025-12-03T16:47:42Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3607798371","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7XET10","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Neurotransmitters (in vesicle) can be transported from the soma to terminals, so neurotransmitter transport is not transmembrane transport. We can simply add 'neurotransmitter loading into synaptic vesicle' is_a 'transmembrane transport'. I don't recall why we want to remove x transport terms. That seems like a big undertaking.","createdAt":"2025-12-03T18:27:01Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3608231284","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7sDnmG","author":{"login":"rozaru"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@pgaudet As _neurotransmitter loading into synaptic vesicle_ is a child of _neurotransmitter transport_ could the 3 children proposed by @raymond91125 be children of _dopamine transport_, tyramine transport and octopamine transport respectively until the transport branch is revised? Currently I have annotated the fly and C.elegans genes to tyramine transport and octopamine transport.","createdAt":"2026-02-25T16:11:23Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3960371590","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7sFufL","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@rozaru Sorry for the confusion. These three terms have never been committed to the ontology pending agreement from @pgaudet. When they do, they will have proper parentage inferred. e.g. ","createdAt":"2026-02-25T17:44:57Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3960924107","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7t7qAZ","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> could the 3 children proposed by @raymond91125 be children of dopamine transport, tyramine transport and octopamine transport respectively until the transport branch is revised? Can we not revise that brach now and obsolete [monoamine transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0015844) and children, or make them 'transmembrane transport' if appropriate ? (this should be done as a separate ticket, but it's not so many annotations, it would be better to fix that sooner rather than later). Otherwise the new terms can go in the ontology. Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2026-03-03T15:33:10Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3991838745","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7t_OiP","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@rozaru The new terms are now in. Sorry for the delay.","createdAt":"2026-03-03T18:16:11Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"THUMBS_UP","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3992774799","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7t_lRv","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@pgaudet I got 114 EXP annotations for X (monoamine) transport. Could you confirm that is what you have in mind?","createdAt":"2026-03-03T18:34:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-3992867951","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws71lZ9x","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please confirm that you are able to do a deep research into the supporting reference of all annotations based on EXP evidence (IDA, IPI, IMP, IGI, IEP) for descendant terms of GO:0015844 monoamine transport to see if any are not about transmembrane transport? If the answer is yes, go ahead and make a table for all such annotations.","createdAt":"2026-03-24T17:47:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4120223601","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws71la3d","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it... Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/23504131152) *— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-03-24T17:48:02Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4120227293","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws71lyqV","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@raymond91125 @pgaudet I've completed the deep research analysis of all EXP evidence annotations (IDA, IPI, IMP, IGI, IEP) for descendant terms of GO:0015844 (monoamine transport). ## Summary **Total EXP annotations analyzed: 317** (from 182 unique PMIDs) **All PMIDs describe transmembrane transport**, but across different membranes: - **307 annotations** (97%): **Plasma membrane transport** (OCT1/2/3, PMAT, DAT, SERT, NET, etc.) - **7 annotations** (2%): **Vesicular membrane transport** - transport INTO synaptic vesicles (VMATs) - **3 annotations** (1%): **Intracellular organelle membrane transport** (ENT3 - lysosomes/mitochondria) ## Annotations NOT About Plasma Membrane Transmembrane Transport | Gene Symbol | Gene Product ID | GO Term ID | GO Term Name | Evidence | PMID | Taxon | Transport Type | |-------------|-----------------|------------|--------------|----------|------|-------|----------------| | Slc18a2 | UniProtKB:Q01827 | GO:0015844 | monoamine transport | IDA | PMID:1438304 | Rat | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | Vmat | UniProtKB:Q8IH57 | GO:0006837 | serotonin transport | IDA | PMID:15849736 | Dmel | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | Vmat | UniProtKB:Q8IH57 | GO:0015844 | monoamine transport | IDA | PMID:15849736 | Dmel | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | Vmat | UniProtKB:Q8IH57 | GO:7770032 | tyramine transport | IDA | PMID:15849736 | Dmel | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | cat-1 | UniProtKB:Q9GNP0 | GO:0006837 | serotonin transport | IDA | PMID:9870940 | C.ele | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | cat-1 | UniProtKB:Q9GNP0 | GO:0015872 | dopamine transport | IDA | PMID:9870940 | C.ele | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | cat-1 | UniProtKB:Q9GNP0 | GO:7770032 | tyramine transport | IDA | PMID:9870940 | C.ele | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | SLC29A3 | UniProtKB:Q9BZD2 | GO:0006837 | serotonin transport | IDA | PMID:19164483 | Human | Intracellular organelle (lysosome/mito) | | SLC29A3 | UniProtKB:Q9BZD2 | GO:0015872 | dopamine transport | IDA | PMID:19164483 | Human | Intracellular organelle (lysosome/mito) | | SLC29A3 | UniProtKB:Q9BZD2 | GO:0015874 | norepinephrine transport | IDA | PMID:19164483 | Human | Intracellular organelle (lysosome/mito) | ## Key Findings ### 1. VMAT Annotations (vesicular transport) The VMAT annotations (Slc18a2, Vmat/dVMAT, cat-1) describe **transport INTO synaptic vesicles** across the vesicle membrane, not across the plasma membrane: - **PMID:1438304**: "A cDNA for a rat vesicular monoamine transporter... The characteristics of [3H]serotonin accumulation by CV-1 cells expressing the cDNA clone suggested sequestration by an intracellular compartment" - **PMID:15849736**: "Vesicular monoamine transporters (VMATs) mediate the transport of dopamine (DA), serotonin (5HT), and other monoamines into secretory vesicles" - **PMID:9870940**: "C. elegans VMAT is associated with synaptic vesicles in approximately 25 neurons" These are still **transmembrane transport** (across a membrane), just not plasma membrane transport. The current "X transport" term definitions are agnostic to which membrane is crossed, so technically these annotations are accurate. ### 2. ENT3/SLC29A3 Annotations (organelle transport) **PMID:19164483** annotates SLC29A3 (ENT3) to monoamine transport terms. However: - ENT3 is primarily a **nucleoside transporter** localized to **lysosomes and mitochondria** - The paper's focus is on nucleoside drug transport, not monoamine biology - Monoamine transport may be a secondary activity - This annotation may warrant review ### 3. Recommendations If the goal is to make "X transport" terms specifically about **plasma membrane transmembrane transport**, then: 1. **7 VMAT annotations** should be moved to more specific terms like: - "X loading into synaptic vesicle" (as proposed in earlier discussion) - The new terms GO:0160310 (dopamine loading into synaptic vesicle), GO:0160311 (tyramine loading into synaptic vesicle), GO:0160312 (octopamine loading into synaptic vesicle) would be appropriate 2. **3 ENT3 annotations** should be reviewed - they may not be appropriate for monoamine transport terms given ENT3's primary role as a nucleoside transporter 3. **307 annotations** (97%) are already correctly about plasma membrane transport and need no changes A detailed RESEARCH.md file has been generated with full analysis of each PMID. --- 🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent** - Model: `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` - Agent harness: claude-code - Triggered by: @raymond91125 - Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/23504131152)","createdAt":"2026-03-24T18:01:06Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4120324757","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws71pkwO","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please how to access the RESEARCH.md file?","createdAt":"2026-03-24T20:58:05Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4121316366","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws71plVX","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it... Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/23511905193) *— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-03-24T20:58:34Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4121318743","viewerDidAuthor":true}],"title":"NTR: terms for tyramine transport and octopamine transport"}

tool_use_result.stdout: {"body":"* **Suggested term label:** tyramine transport * **Definition (free text)** The directed movement of tyramine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Tyramine is a monoamine neurotransmitter occurring predominantly in invertebrates neuronal as well as in non-neuronal tissues. * **Reference, in format PMID:#######** PMID:9870940 (C.elegans) PMID:15849736 (Dmel) PMID:32305263 (review, Dmel) * **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term** Vmat (Dmel) cat-1 (C.elegans) * **Parent term(s)** GO:0015844 monoamine transport * **Children terms (if applicable)** Should any existing terms that should be moved underneath this new proposed term? The existing term GO:0061545 tyramine secretion will be a child of the new term --- * **Suggested term label:** octopamine transport * **Definition (free text)** The directed movement of octopamine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Octopamine is a monoamine neurotransmitter occurring predominantly in invertebrates neuronal as well as in non-neuronal tissues. * **Reference, in format PMID:#######** PMID:9870940 (C.elegans) PMID:15849736 (Dmel) PMID:32305263 (review, Dmel) * **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term** Vmat (Dmel) cat-1 (C.elegans) * **Parent term(s)** GO:0015844 monoamine transport * **Children terms (if applicable)** Should any existing terms that should be moved underneath this new proposed term? Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/23504131152) *— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-03-24T17:48:02Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4120227293","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws71lyqV","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@raymond91125 @pgaudet I've completed the deep research analysis of all EXP evidence annotations (IDA, IPI, IMP, IGI, IEP) for descendant terms of GO:0015844 (monoamine transport). ## Summary **Total EXP annotations analyzed: 317** (from 182 unique PMIDs) **All PMIDs describe transmembrane transport**, but across different membranes: - **307 annotations** (97%): **Plasma membrane transport** (OCT1/2/3, PMAT, DAT, SERT, NET, etc.) - **7 annotations** (2%): **Vesicular membrane transport** - transport INTO synaptic vesicles (VMATs) - **3 annotations** (1%): **Intracellular organelle membrane transport** (ENT3 - lysosomes/mitochondria) ## Annotations NOT About Plasma Membrane Transmembrane Transport | Gene Symbol | Gene Product ID | GO Term ID | GO Term Name | Evidence | PMID | Taxon | Transport Type | |-------------|-----------------|------------|--------------|----------|------|-------|----------------| | Slc18a2 | UniProtKB:Q01827 | GO:0015844 | monoamine transport | IDA | PMID:1438304 | Rat | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | Vmat | UniProtKB:Q8IH57 | GO:0006837 | serotonin transport | IDA | PMID:15849736 | Dmel | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | Vmat | UniProtKB:Q8IH57 | GO:0015844 | monoamine transport | IDA | PMID:15849736 | Dmel | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | Vmat | UniProtKB:Q8IH57 | GO:7770032 | tyramine transport | IDA | PMID:15849736 | Dmel | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | cat-1 | UniProtKB:Q9GNP0 | GO:0006837 | serotonin transport | IDA | PMID:9870940 | C.ele | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | cat-1 | UniProtKB:Q9GNP0 | GO:0015872 | dopamine transport | IDA | PMID:9870940 | C.ele | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | cat-1 | UniProtKB:Q9GNP0 | GO:7770032 | tyramine transport | IDA | PMID:9870940 | C.ele | Vesicular membrane (into synaptic vesicle) | | SLC29A3 | UniProtKB:Q9BZD2 | GO:0006837 | serotonin transport | IDA | PMID:19164483 | Human | Intracellular organelle (lysosome/mito) | | SLC29A3 | UniProtKB:Q9BZD2 | GO:0015872 | dopamine transport | IDA | PMID:19164483 | Human | Intracellular organelle (lysosome/mito) | | SLC29A3 | UniProtKB:Q9BZD2 | GO:0015874 | norepinephrine transport | IDA | PMID:19164483 | Human | Intracellular organelle (lysosome/mito) | ## Key Findings ### 1. VMAT Annotations (vesicular transport) The VMAT annotations (Slc18a2, Vmat/dVMAT, cat-1) describe **transport INTO synaptic vesicles** across the vesicle membrane, not across the plasma membrane: - **PMID:1438304**: "A cDNA for a rat vesicular monoamine transporter... The characteristics of [3H]serotonin accumulation by CV-1 cells expressing the cDNA clone suggested sequestration by an intracellular compartment" - **PMID:15849736**: "Vesicular monoamine transporters (VMATs) mediate the transport of dopamine (DA), serotonin (5HT), and other monoamines into secretory vesicles" - **PMID:9870940**: "C. elegans VMAT is associated with synaptic vesicles in approximately 25 neurons" These are still **transmembrane transport** (across a membrane), just not plasma membrane transport. The current "X transport" term definitions are agnostic to which membrane is crossed, so technically these annotations are accurate. ### 2. ENT3/SLC29A3 Annotations (organelle transport) **PMID:19164483** annotates SLC29A3 (ENT3) to monoamine transport terms. However: - ENT3 is primarily a **nucleoside transporter** localized to **lysosomes and mitochondria** - The paper's focus is on nucleoside drug transport, not monoamine biology - Monoamine transport may be a secondary activity - This annotation may warrant review ### 3. Recommendations If the goal is to make "X transport" terms specifically about **plasma membrane transmembrane transport**, then: 1. **7 VMAT annotations** should be moved to more specific terms like: - "X loading into synaptic vesicle" (as proposed in earlier discussion) - The new terms GO:0160310 (dopamine loading into synaptic vesicle), GO:0160311 (tyramine loading into synaptic vesicle), GO:0160312 (octopamine loading into synaptic vesicle) would be appropriate 2. **3 ENT3 annotations** should be reviewed - they may not be appropriate for monoamine transport terms given ENT3's primary role as a nucleoside transporter 3. **307 annotations** (97%) are already correctly about plasma membrane transport and need no changes A detailed RESEARCH.md file has been generated with full analysis of each PMID. --- 🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent** - Model: `claude-opus-4-5-20251101` - Agent harness: claude-code - Triggered by: @raymond91125 - Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/23504131152)","createdAt":"2026-03-24T18:01:06Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4120324757","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws71pkwO","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@dragon-ai-agent please how to access the RESEARCH.md file?","createdAt":"2026-03-24T20:58:05Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[{"content":"EYES","users":{"totalCount":1}}],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4121316366","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws71plVX","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"🤖 Working on it... Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/23511905193) *— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-03-24T20:58:34Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31143#issuecomment-4121318743","viewerDidAuthor":true}],"title":"NTR: terms for tyramine transport and octopamine transport"}
